title.explorer: A New Way to Run the Economy!

summary.scholar: President Reagan's economic policy, dubbed 'Reaganomics,' centered on supply-side economics: cutting income tax rates (the top rate dropped from 70% to 28%), reducing government regulation, and increasing military spending. Supporters credited these policies with sparking economic growth after the 1981-1982 recession. Critics argued they primarily benefited the wealthy and tripled the national debt from $1 trillion to $2.9 trillion. The debate over supply-side economics continues to shape American politics.
summary.explorer: President Reagan had a big idea about the economy called Reaganomics. He cut taxes A LOT (especially for rich people and businesses) and said the money would 'trickle down' to everyone else. The economy did grow a lot, but the government also went deep into debt. People STILL argue about whether Reaganomics was a good idea or not!

significance.scholar: Reaganomics fundamentally reoriented American economic policy toward lower taxes and deregulation, shaping the conservative economic agenda that persists today.
significance.explorer: Whether you think Reaganomics was great or terrible, it changed how Americans think about taxes and government spending. We're still debating it!

sources.citation: Miller Center: U.S. Presidents and Key Events
